mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations. Persistently low FEDFUNDS levels are associated with positive forward S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors continue to favor risk assets over low-yielding fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption pullback and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, or Rank IC conditional on trigger activation is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that compounds through the economy over a quarter, driving sustained equity appreciation. Neither series alone captures this effect; the interaction of consumption and residential investment momentum is required to signal durable demand-led growth.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month annualized growth positive simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint positive-growth episodes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce forward 60-day SPX returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) over the evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and forward corporate revenue growth, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets incorporate incremental production data.
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX return is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment is low and payrolls continue growing, robust nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth signals labor market tightness that eventually pressures wage costs and margins, producing weaker S&P 500 returns over the following quarter than payroll strength would otherwise suggest, as markets price in future Fed tightening risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 150k
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During periods meeting the low-unemployment/strong-payroll trigger, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the predicted negative mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than headline CPI inflation, real short-term rates increase, tightening financial conditions and raising the discount rate applied to equities. The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS acceleration outpacing CPIAUCSL growth signals a negative regime shift for equities over the following month that neither series predicts in isolation.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS exceeds 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL inflation rate
failure_condition: Episodes where FEDFUNDS growth outpaces CPI inflation do not show significantly lower forward 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below a 2% annualized threshold signals weakening aggregate demand that translates into slower corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as equity risk premia widen in anticipation of a slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth falls below 2%
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following sub-2% GDPC1 growth prints are not significantly lower than the full-sample average (p > 0.05), or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in payroll growth (PAYEMS) alongside a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) within the same reporting month constitutes a coincident labor deterioration signal that markets react to sharply within days, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week. Neither indicator alone reliably triggers this short-horizon reaction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change negative AND UNRATE month-over-month change positive
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where both PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase co-occur, or the joint signal shows no significant improvement in Rank IC over either single-series signal.
